comment.body: I think there is a chance the bear party is over...i see huge trades after hours in SH,SPXU,SPXS SDS as we saw after hours on Friday...either they are adding or selling...maybe news event coming in.... the trades whilst not exactly the sae size where big round numbers very close in size to Fridays trade. Here you have the SPXS example...15.5 mill Friday 15.1 million today. 2 largest trades ever in that name....others the same. I would think it was people removing the friday positions...but maybe they are doubling down LOL.... comment.body: She said reversal at 5150 EXCEPT vola is relative low, which it was. Because when dealers are short delta, they become even shorter delta when market falls more and so they would need to buy spx to not end up exposed. But when vola falls too fast this is reducing or even eliminating the previous effect, dealers become not shorter and thus dont need to buy spx for hedging. BUT maybe the downwards dynamic was also just flow-driven
comment.raw_body: She said reversal at 5150 EXCEPT vola is relative low, which it was. Because when dealers are short delta, they become even shorter delta when market falls more and so they would need to buy spx to not end up exposed. But when vola falls too fast this is reducing or even eliminating the previous effect, dealers become not shorter and thus dont need to buy spx for hedging. comment.body: Were there any other blocks that could be determined as “buys” on any of the long ETF’s or Mag7 names? If profit is being taken on leveraged and volatility instruments then it could be just volatility selling. Usually if there are multiple legs down in a correction volatility spikes less and less with each subsequent leg down. What kind of bounce would you be expecting here? More than 5300?

comment.body: Supportive OpEx -> brief (how brief, unknown) period of "post OpEx buy back flows" -> resumption of volatility / decline was my takeaway. Up for interpretation, it seems.
